title: Metric Matching Enhancement with Known Metric and Formula Comparison
story: As a data analyst using the Metrics Analyst, I want the system to automatically detect and categorize duplicate or conflicting metrics using AI-powered matching, so that I can quickly identify true new metrics, review conflicts, and avoid adding duplicates to my glossary.
description:
The Service 2 response now includes Known Metric, Known Formula, and Known Metric by Formula fields that enable intelligent metric matching. Three scenarios need to be handled with alert icons and tooltips:

**Scenario 1 — Name Match, Different Formula:**
`Known Metric` is NOT NULL AND `Known Metric by Formula` is NULL.
Tooltip: 'This metric has the same name as an existing metric but is calculated differently.'

**Scenario 2 — Formula Match, Different Name:**
`Known Metric` is NULL AND `Known Metric by Formula` is NOT NULL.
Tooltip: 'This metric matches another formula. It could be a candidate to merge or you could have 2 different metrics with the same calculation needing further review.'

**Scenario 3 — Validated Duplicate (Auto-Ignore):**
`Known Metric` = `Known Metric by Formula`.
Automatically move to the Ignored section. The AI has confirmed this is a duplicate.

Also rename 'Ignored Facts' to 'Ignored' (remove the word 'Fact').
acceptance_criteria: Scenario 1: Alert icon with name-match tooltip appears when Known Metric is set but Known Metric by Formula is null
Scenario 2: Alert icon with formula-match tooltip appears when Known Metric is null but Known Metric by Formula is set
Scenario 3: Metrics where Known Metric equals Known Metric by Formula are auto-placed in the Ignored section
Alert tooltips clearly explain the matching scenario to the user
Section label changed from 'Ignored Facts' to 'Ignored'
Auto-ignored metrics increment the Ignored count card automatically
ux_changes: Added alert icons with descriptive tooltips for metric matching scenarios. Auto-ignore moves validated duplicates to the Ignored section. Renamed 'Ignored Facts' to 'Ignored'.
middleware_changes: Added comparison logic for Known Metric, Known Formula, and Known Metric by Formula fields. Auto-sets the ignore flag when Known Formula equals Known Metric by Formula (case-insensitive).
data_model_changes: The ApiResponseMetric model was updated to store the new Known Metric, Known Formula, and Known Metric by Formula fields from the Service 2 response.
implementation_notes: The auto-ignore logic uses case-insensitive string comparison: `strtolower($metricData['Known Formula']) == strtolower($metricData['Known Metric by Formula'])`. The system also compares against metric aliases, not just names, which enables broader duplicate detection. Testing revealed an edge case where the AI service was flagging a metric name match that didn't actually exist in the glossary — this was traced to alias matching behavior.
comment_summary: Developer implemented the three matching scenarios and auto-ignore logic. Testing showed the comparison also works against metric aliases. Ryan tested 4 different scenarios without finding logic breaks. Minor edge case found with alias matching was noted for AI service enhancement.
